title: Annotations Mitigate Post-Training Mode Collapse
gist: Apple/CMU show that supervised fine-tuning induces semantic mode collapse that worsens with scale, and propose annotation-anchored training that pairs pretraining documents with semantic annotations, achieving 6x less diversity collapse than SFT while improving with scale.

insights.claim: Supervised fine-tuning improves instruction-following but induces semantic mode collapse by biasing models toward low-entropy fine-tuning data at the expense of the high-entropy pretraining distribution.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Quantifies a hidden cost of the standard post-training recipe: instruction-following is bought with lost output diversity.
insights.quote: Post-training (via supervised fine-tuning) improves instruction-following, but often induces semantic mode collapse by biasing models toward low-entropy fine-tuning data at the expense of the high-entropy pretraining distribution.

insights.claim: The trade-off between instruction-following and semantic diversity worsens as model scale increases, contrary to the assumption that scale alone alleviates such issues.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Warns that diversity loss is a growing liability for frontier-scale models, relevant to creative and synthetic-data use cases.
insights.quote: Crucially, we find this trade-off worsens with scale.
insights.locator: Crucially, we find this trade-off worsens

insights.claim: Annotation-anchored training pretrains on documents paired with semantic annotations to induce a rich annotation distribution reflecting the full breadth of pretraining data, then preserves that distribution during post-training so diverse annotations can anchor generation at inference time.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Offers a route to retain instruction-following without sacrificing diversity, valuable for synthetic data generation and exploration.
insights.quote: we pretrain on documents paired with semantic annotations, inducing a rich annotation distribution that reflects the full breadth of pretraining data, and we preserve this distribution during post-training

insights.claim: Models trained with annotation-anchored training attain 6x less diversity collapse than models trained with standard SFT, and the benefit improves with scale.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: A 6x diversity improvement that scales positively is a differentiated post-training capability worth monitoring competitively.
insights.quote: models trained with annotation-anchored training can attain 6× less diversity collapse than models trained with SFT, and improve with scale
insights.locator: We find that models trained with annotation-anchored training
